title: ranunculus monspeliacus proles albicans
intro: Ranunculus monspeliacus proles albicans, also known as White Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ranunculus. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
